title: Stenorrhynchos cinnabarinum
intro: Stenorrhynchos cinnabarinum, commonly known as Scarlet Pimpernel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the orchid family, characterized by its unique, vibrant red flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising treatment for various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stenorrhynchos cinnabarinum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
taxonomy: Stenorrhynchos cinnabarinum is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. The Orchidaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Orchidoideae subfamily, to which Stenorrhynchos cinnabarinum is assigned. This subfamily is characterized by its terrestrial habit and a wide range of flower shapes and colors. The genus Stenorrhynchos is a small group of orchids that are native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Stenorrhynchos cinnabarinum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Orchidaceae family.
uses: Stenorrhynchos cinnabarinum has been traditionally used in folk medicine, although its effectiveness and safety are not extensively documented.
